name: Byodo-In Temple
tagline: A full-scale replica of a 900-year-old Kyoto temple tucked against the Ko'olau cliffs — ring the 3-ton bell for blessings.
bestTime: Early morning for misty mountain backdrop and fewer crowds — golden hour light is stunning for photos
kamaaina: Kamaaina rate: $4 adults, $3 seniors, $2 kids (vs $7/$6/$4 general) with Hawaii ID. No cash — card/contactless only
talkStory: Built in 1968 to honor 100 years of Japanese immigration to Hawaii. Ring the sacred bon-sho bell before entering — tradition says it brings happiness and long life. Inside sits a 9-foot golden Lotus Buddha. Buy $1 fish food at the gift shop to feed the koi (some are over 100 years old) — don't bring outside food, it makes them sick. Wild peacocks roam the grounds but can be feisty during mating season. The temple sits in the Valley of the Temples Memorial Park, a multi-faith cemetery at the base of the Ko'olau mountains. Allow 1-2 hours. Was a filming location for Lost.
